[color=184,35,35,255]Health[/color] defines how much vitality a character has. If it drops to 0, the character will die.

[color=192,176,27,255]Stamina[/color] is the yellow bar and describes short term energy, how much a character can do before before taking a short rest. If your stamina drops too low, you can press '[color=207,192,178,255]r[/color]' to spend a little time regaining it.

[color=176,101,43,255]Fatigue[/color] is the orange bar and it describes long term energy loss, it is increased when character regains stamina through resting. To reduce fatigue, character will have to sleep, preferably in bed, by pressing '[color=207,192,178,255]R[/color]'. If fatigue goes up to 100, the character will lose consciousness and automatically go to sleep.

[color=53,176,43,255]Hunger[/color] defines how much the character is lacking food. If hunger bar drops below 0, starvation level will increase, and hunger level will start dropping from 100 again.

[color=158,27,27,255]Starvation[/color] just like dehydration level reduces every statistic by 2 for every level of starvation. If starvation level reaches 4, the character won't be able to travel on the world map. If starvation level reaches 5, the character will die.

[color=43,169,176,255]Thirst[/color] defines how much the character is lacking water. If thirst drops below 0, dehydration level will increase, and thirst level will start dropping from 100 again.

[color=143,91,170,255]Dehydration[/color] just like starvation level reduces every statistic by 2 for every level of dehydration. If dehydration level reaches 4, the character won't be able to travel on the world map. If dehydration level reaches 5, the character will die.

[color=138,7,7,255]Bloodthirst[/color] defines how much the character is lacking blood. If bloodthirst drops below 0, dehydration level will increase, and bloodthirst level will start dropping from 100 again.

[color=143,91,170,255]Dehydration[/color] level reduces the chance of successful attacks and many other actions by 10%. If dehydration level reaches 10, the character will die.

Like to do your thing with little consequence? Want to explore the world at your own pace? Build a hut in the forest while the world around you burns to the ground? This game mode is for you.

- Drops all items from backpack and respawns in home settlement after death.
- Your character doesn't age.
- Your character can't die from starvation or dehydration.
- Skills require 200% more effort to advance.
- You can only begin as an orphan.
- Scoring disabled.

Do you appreciate things that require a bit of effort to acquire? Do you like the high-stakes combat of traditional roguelikes? Do you wish your existence could change the world in a meaningful way? This is the recommended game mode.

- Permanent character death.
- You can continue in the same world with a new character.
- No penalty to skill gain.
- You can begin as part of an existing family.

If you believe meta progression is for scrubs, or you wish to take on the game's highest possible challenge, this is it. Recommended for masochists.

- When you die, the world dies with you.

Sneaking allows characters to walk quietly, unheard by nearby beings, but it does not have any effect if they remain in the line of sight.

Sneaking attempt is performed automatically when walking, and its chance of success is determined by distance from every being and their opposing hearing checks.

Sneaking may also determine if you get noticed while pickpocketing, robbing, and stealing.

Hearing allows characters to notice the movement they don't see, which is indicated by the red flashing exclamation sign.

Hearing attempt is performed when another being makes a move, and its chance of success is reduced by distance and Sneaking value.

Hit property improves the chance to hit an opponent when performing a basic attack and is an opposite property to Parry.

Hit check is performed as follows:
[color=255,246,143,255](Hit + 1d10) / (Parry + 1d10) = Chance to Hit %[/color]
Minimum chance to hit is 10%. The visible range is between all your equipped weapons' minimum and maximum hit values.

Parry property improves the chance to avoid getting hit by an opponent during a basic attack and is an opposite property to Hit. Abilities cannot be parried.

Hit check is performed as follows:
[color=255,246,143,255](Hit + 1d10) / (Parry + 1d10) = Chance to Hit %[/color]
Max chance to parry is 90%.

Parry can occur only 1 (2 with shield) times between attacks, which means performing your attack resets the counter. Both abilities that deal damage and regular attacks reset the counter, and drawing blood is unnecessary.

Resistances cover specific areas of your body and reduce damage received from different sources. There are four major areas where you can get hit, and for each of these locations, only some of the items you wear provide cover.

[color=255,246,143,255]Head (16%)[/color] - covered by items in [color=215,206,123,255]head cloth[/color], [color=215,206,123,255]head armor[/color], and [color=215,206,123,255]amulet[/color] slots.
[color=255,246,143,255]Torso (36%)[/color] - covered by items in [color=215,206,123,255]torso cloth[/color], [color=215,206,123,255]torso armor[/color], [color=215,206,123,255]belt[/color], and [color=215,206,123,255]cloak[/color] slots. 
[color=255,246,143,255]Arms (28%)[/color] - covered by items in [color=215,206,123,255]gloves[/color] and [color=215,206,123,255]both ring[/color] slots.
[color=255,246,143,255]Legs (20%)[/color] - covered by items in [color=215,206,123,255]legs cloth[/color], [color=215,206,123,255]legs armor[/color], and [color=215,206,123,255]boots[/color] slots.

Additionally, wielding a [color=215,206,123,255]shield[/color] in the offhand slot provides resistance to each location mentioned above.

Each resistance point reduces the damage of the corresponding type by 1 point up to minimum damage value.
[color=215,206,123,255]Minimum damage[/color] depends on [color=215,206,123,255]combat disadventage[/color]: attacks from the front deals min. of [color=255,246,143,255]10%[/color] of rolled damage, flanking attacks [color=255,246,143,255]20%[/color] and backstabbing attacks as much as [color=255,246,143,255]30%[/color].

Damage dealt by [color=215,206,123,255]weather[/color] and [color=215,206,123,255]environment conditions[/color] can be reduced to 0 damage by resistance, ignoring minimum damage.

statistics.description:
[color=98,116,133,255]Strength[/color]

- Improves weapon damage by 1 for every 2 points for axe, sword, mace, improvised, natural, and thrown weapons and 1 for every 4 points for unarmed.
- Improves chance to parry and hit depending on weapon type.
- Increases available carry weight.
- Reduces the time required for certain actions like Mining, Woodcutting, Harvesting, Threshing, and Digging.
- Reduces stamina cost of actions like Dragging and Climbing.
statistics.name: Strength

statistics.description:
[color=98,116,133,255]Dexterity[/color]

- Improves weapon damage by 1 for every 2 points for daggers, polearms, bows and crossbows and 1 for every 4 points for unarmed.
- Improves chance to parry and hit depending on weapon type.
- Increases Sneaking.
- Reduces the time to Scavenge corpses.
statistics.name: Dexterity

statistics.description:
[color=98,116,133,255]Endurance[/color]

- Improves physical resistance by 1 for every 2 points.
- Reduces fatigue gain when resting.
- Increases maximum health by 5 per point (only Player Character).
- Reduces stamina cost of making a base attack, movement and actions like Mining, Woodcutting, Harvesting, Threshing, and Digging.
- Determines bonus health regeneration during long sleep and rest until healed actions.
statistics.name: Endurance

statistics.description:
[color=98,116,133,255]Intelligence[/color]

- Increases Magic Power by 1 for every 2 points.
- Increases Sight range by 1 for every 10 points.
- Reduces the purchase prices by 0.5% for every point (max 50%).
- Increases the chance to recover resources from salvaged items by 0.2% per point for each used resource.
- Reduces crafting time by about 1% per point depending on tool quality.
statistics.name: Intelligence

statistics.description:
[color=98,116,133,255]Willpower[/color]

- Improves all non-physical resistances by 1 for every 2 points.
- Increases fatigue reduction during sleep.
- Increases amount of stamina regained through Rest action.
- Improves health regeneration per tick.
- Improves Hearing.
statistics.name: Willpower

action_type.name: Eat
action_type.description: Eat food to increase satiation and reduce hunger.

action_type.name: Drink
action_type.description: Drink the liquid.

action_type.name: Drink
action_type.description: Drink the liquid.

action_type.name: Rest
action_type.description: Rest for 1 turn and regain stamina at the cost of fatigue.

action_type.name: Rest Until Healed
action_type.description: Attempt to rest until health is fully regeneration. Will stop in case of danger.

action_type.name: Sleep
action_type.description: Sleep to reduce fatigue. Sleeping at the same tile as Resting Place will allow you to recover quicker.

action_type.name: Move
action_type.description: Move to selected tile.

action_type.name: Flee
action_type.description: Attempt to flee from the enemy.

action_type.name: Melee Attack
action_type.description: Walk close to the target and perform melee attack using all weapons.

action_type.name: Range Attack
action_type.description: Stay in place and perform a ranged weapon attack if the target is in range.

action_type.name: Equip
action_type.description: Equip selected item to allowed equipment slot.

action_type.name: Unequip
action_type.description: Unequip selected item from equipment and place it in your backpack.

action_type.name: Pick Up
action_type.description: Pick up selected item from the ground and place it in your backpack.

action_type.name: Drop
action_type.description: Drop the item on the ground below your feet.

action_type.name: Stash
action_type.description: Stash an item in a nearby storage of your family.

action_type.name: Craft Item
action_type.description: Create a new item using selected resources.

action_type.name: Build
action_type.description: Build structures in the environment.

action_type.name: Salvage
action_type.description: Salvage an item for a chance to recover random resource.

action_type.name: Use Ability
action_type.description: Use one of your many abilities.

action_type.name: Fill Container
action_type.description: Fill in the container with a liquid from a nearby source.

action_type.name: Use
action_type.description: Use the item for an effect.

action_type.name: Light Up
action_type.description: Fire up this source of light.

action_type.name: Extinguish
action_type.description: Extinguish this source of light.

action_type.name: Split Stack
action_type.description: Split this stack of items into two smaller stacks.

action_type.name: Clean Up
action_type.description: Wash away the blood.

action_type.name: Open / Close
action_type.description: Open or close this entrance.

action_type.name: Clear Container
action_type.description: Clear the container of any liquid.

action_type.name: Wait
action_type.description: Just wait.

action_type.name: Drag
action_type.description:
Drag an item after you as you walk. It will greatly increase the stamina burn on movement depending on the weight of the item.

Provides Athletics experience up to level 5.

action_type.name: Produce
action_type.description: Use production action to extract some items or resources.

action_type.name: Look At
action_type.description: Rotate to look in the chosen direction. You may also hold the [color=192,176,27,255]left alt[/color] key and click a tile to rotate in that direction.

action_type.name: Sleep Until Evening
action_type.description: Character sleeps until late evening, reducing fatigue, and gaining bonus to health regeneration while performing this action based on the duration of rest, with a maximum value based on endurance.

action_type.name: Sleep Until Morning
action_type.description: Character sleeps until early morning, reducing fatigue, and gaining bonus to health regeneration while performing this action based on the duration of rest, with a maximum value based on endurance.

action_type.name: Work
action_type.description: Work at the building to produce valuable items and resources.

action_type.name: Converse
action_type.description: Attempt to talk to the selected being.

action_type.name: Enter Place
action_type.description: Go up or down depending on where the exit leads.

action_type.name: Trade
action_type.description: Attempt to exchange goods with the selected being.

action_type.name: Free Attack
action_type.description: Automatic attack that doesn't require time.

action_type.name: Upgrade Item
action_type.description: Replace resources in existing items.

action_type.name: Throw
action_type.description: Throw an item laying on the ground. The distance and damage done when the object hits something will be determined by your strength and weight of the object. Stamina cost varies depending on Endurance and the object's weight.

action_type.name: Climb
action_type.description: Climb up to a higher ground. It needs a walkable tile in the higher area.

action_type.name: Jump Down
action_type.description: Jump down to the lower area. It may cause pain or death depending on the height.

action_type.name: Stop Dragging
action_type.description: Stop dragging the item.

action_type.name: Pickpocket
action_type.description: Attempt to steal some coins from the character. The success depends on opposite Sneaking and Hearing test. Don't get noticed!

action_type.name: Rob
action_type.description:
Rob stash of a family in a settlement to take some of its resources.

Attempting this action will make a check of Sneaking vs. Hearing of every nearby character to check if you've been noticed while committing this action.

To improve your chances of being unnoticed, position yourself as far as you can from other characters and increase your Sneaking.

action_type.name: Pass Wall
action_type.description: Pass through 1-tile collidable object if there's a walkable tile behind. Costs 40 stamina.

action_type.name: Track
action_type.description: Track down closest being.

action_type.name: Tame
action_type.description: Use your willpower to attempt to tame an animal. The action requires an empty companion slot and takes 2 turns.

action_type.name: Pet
action_type.description: Look at those eyes, how can you not pet that animal?

action_type.name: Bury
action_type.description: Bury the corpse in the ground.

action_type.name: Harness Power
action_type.description: Gather souls of the dead lingering in this region at the speed of 10 per turn. When performed in a settlement it will be treated as an unforgiveable crime due to imposing fear to its inhabitants.

action_type.name: Take Control
action_type.description: Take control of this being.

action_type.name: Soul Tithe
action_type.description: Demand tribute of 5 Sullied Embers as a settlement's sacrifice. This action is available at any settlement with less than 10 military units and at least 20 population.

action_type.name: Resurrect
action_type.description:
Reconstruct a dead person's body and soul to join you as a companion. Use this option in the Being Knowledge tab.

Requires 1 Prismatic Abyss and an empty compaion slot.

action_type.name: Lock Item
action_type.description: Lock the item, preventing it from accidental salvage or selling.

action_type.name: Turn Vampire
action_type.description: As a Vampire, you can turn other denizens of a controlled settlement or your party members into lesser vampires. Consumes 100 units of Vampiric Blood.

action_type.name: Order Hold
action_type.description: Orders your companion to wait for you in the area.

action_type.name: Order Follow
action_type.description: Order your companion to follow you.

race.name: Human
race.description:
[color=155,155,155,255]Humans were the most common being in the world once, but these days are long gone. The population decimated by constant wars is now just a shadow of its former glory. Representatives of this race are extremely adaptable to the situation, but their history was marked heavily by external influence. As the War of Worlds has ended, a great empire has been formed, where single human family clings to the seat of power to this day.[/color]

All statistics +1
Gets hungry 50% slower

[color=130,163,175,255]Items:[/color] Knife, Bandage
[color=130,163,175,255]Recipes:[/color] Fishing rod

race.name: Dark dwarf
race.description:
[color=155,155,155,255]Dark dwarves are a subrace of dwarves completely corrupted by greed. They prefer to build their incredible keeps very deep below the ground or near volcanoes. Beings of these race are known for being cruel and malevolent.[/color]

Endurance +2
Willpower +2
Infravision: see in the dark.
Tinkerer: double chance to learn new recipe when salvaging.

[color=130,163,175,255]Items:[/color] Pickaxe, Belt
[color=130,163,175,255]Recipes:[/color] Pickaxe

race.name: Goblin
race.description:
[color=155,155,155,255]Goblins are small greenskin creatures known for their ruthlessness. Some say they are shorter orc cousins. They form tribes and prefer to live in the mountains. Their greed pushes them to challenge dwarves for their territory constantly. From time to time, they organize in hordes that swarm even the human empire.[/color]

Dexterity +3, Endurance +1
Infravision: see in the dark.
Sneaking +10

[color=130,163,175,255]Items:[/color] Rags
[color=130,163,175,255]Recipes:[/color] Rags

race.name: Dark elf
race.description:
[color=155,155,155,255]A long time ago dark elves were living in the forests with their brethren, but they were corrupted by dark gods. Now they occupy huge underground cities. Their controversial religious practices and rituals make them feared and dreaded. They despise most other races for opposing their rightful place among them.[/color]

Dexterity +2, Intelligence +1, Willpower +1
Infravision: see in the dark.
Sight range +1

[color=130,163,175,255]Items:[/color] Dagger
[color=130,163,175,255]Recipes:[/color] Short bow

race.name: Vampire
race.description:
[color=155,155,155,255]Vampires are dead beings of living races, cursed to wander the world till the end of times. They don't eat regular food and don't drink water. They feed by drinking the blood of the living to sustain their immortal bodies. Vampires have formed an enclave in the ruins of Black Sun Castle, where they scheme to take over the world and enslave the living like cattle.[/color]

Strength +1, Endurance +2, Intelligence +1
Thirst and hunger is replaced by bloodlust.
Infravision: see in the dark.

[color=130,163,175,255]Items:[/color] Cloak, Hood
[color=130,163,175,255]Recipes:[/color] Cloak

race.name: Troll
race.description:
[color=155,155,155,255]Trolls are huge humanoid beasts. Not many beings of this race are considered smart, but they make up for it with brutal, physical power. Trolls have the strength to tear smaller beings apart in the blink of an eye. They are forever hungry and possess very potent regenerative powers.[/color]

Strength +3, Endurance +1, Intelligence -2
Regenerates health 20% faster.
Gets hungry 50% faster.

[color=130,163,175,255]Items:[/color] Club
[color=130,163,175,255]Recipes:[/color] Club

race.name: Orc
race.description:
[color=155,155,155,255]Orcs are green. Orcs are mean. Orcs want to smash, bash and eat. They form hordes led by the most brutal chieftains of their kind to raid other races for loot. Their physical appearance is second only to trolls and giants.[/color]

Strength +2, Endurance +2
Hit bonus +1 on all weapons

[color=130,163,175,255]Items:[/color] Hand axe
[color=130,163,175,255]Recipes:[/color] Hatchet

race.name: Lich
race.description:
[color=155,155,155,255]Liches are powerful mages that cheated death. There are not many of them in the world, but most are immensely powerful. Their center of power is in the Black Pyramid of Sareth where God Pharaoh Sandaver rules over the dead. The power that allows these beings to defy death is shrouded in mystery.[/color]

Intelligence +2, Willpower +2
Thirst and hunger is replaced by life essence.
Death aura dealing 1 death damage to everything nearby every turn.

[color=130,163,175,255]Items:[/color] Quarterstaff
[color=130,163,175,255]Recipes:[/color] Mask

race.name: Rasimi
race.description:
[color=155,155,155,255]Rasimi are cat-like people. They are many sub-races among them, having common parts with lions, panthers, tigers or regular house cats. They live deep in the jungles of Tar'Var. They are individuals, loners and prefer seclusion over contact with others, as they considering other races untrustworthy. The feeling is often mutual.[/color]

Dexterity +2, Strength +1, Endurance +1
Infravision: see in the dark.
Move 10% faster

[color=130,163,175,255]Items:[/color] Dagger, Shirt
[color=130,163,175,255]Recipes:[/color] Dagger

component_type.name: Ability user
component_type.description:
Ability user component allows entities to use abilities during combat. One of the selected [color=192,176,27,255]ability[/color] will be chosen every attack when usage is available.

Required components: Actor, Artificial Intelligence, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Achievements
component_type.description: Component for achievements calculation, matters only for Player entity.

component_type.name: Actor
component_type.description:
Actor can take different actions in the game, like cutting wood, moving, fighting, etc.

Required components: Artificial Intelligence, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Armor
component_type.description:
Adds resistances to damage types. [color=192,176,27,255]Parry[/color] on armor component adds up to weapon parry. Every parry point increases sum of parry value by 10%.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: AI
component_type.description:
Artificial Intelligence component is necessary for many entities that 'live' in the world. The base setting is to define tags for [color=192,176,27,255]allies[/color] and [color=192,176,27,255]enemies[/color] in the world. Check [color=192,176,27,255]Tag component[/color] description for more information about tags.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Background
component_type.description:
Background component allows defining an RGB background [color=192,176,27,255]color[/color] and optionally a walking sound ([color=192,176,27,255]noise[/color]) for entities that enter the tile. It is commonly used by lowest tiles on the map without Position2D component and different collidable entities like walls. Noise can have [color=192,176,27,255]intensity[/color] defined which determines how much noise someone will make when walking the tile.

Required components: Name.

component_type.name: Block light
component_type.description:
Defines if entity blocks light source or vision.

Required components: Name.

component_type.name: Bounty
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Builder
component_type.description:
This component allows entities to build thing in the world. Currently disabled.

Required components: Actor, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Chained
component_type.description:
Chain multiple entities together to work as a single multi-tile entity.
[color=192,176,27,255]Leader[/color] defines if specific bodypart is leader. Leader drives the movement, and when killed, kills entire entity.
[color=192,176,27,255]Snake[/color] snake bodyparts move into the previous bodypart in the list movement position on move.
[color=192,176,27,255]Main Body[/color] entity also dies if main body is killed.
[color=192,176,27,255]Regrow Delay[/color] time (in turns) after which the specific body part regrow.
[color=192,176,27,255]Regrow Multiplier[/color] if regrow multiplier is bigger then 1, then regrow will trigger more bodyparts, and more parts will grow at once. Limit is (body party count) * (multiplier). Extra parts need space near the leader or mainbody to work.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Collidable
component_type.description:
Collidable entity blocks the tile, not allowing other collidable entities to enter. In other words, no two entities with collidable component can occupy a single tile.

Required components: Name.

component_type.name: Consumer
component_type.description:
Entity with this component requires food and drink. The actual consumption may differ depending on the race. For example, vampires consume blood, while liches feed on death damage dealt. Race for entities other than the player may be decided based on Tag component.

Required components: Actor, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Container
component_type.description:
Container allows entity to hold something in a limited or unlimited volume. Currently only liquid container [color=192,176,27,255]type[/color] is supported. Capacity determines how many units of selected type can be held in the container, where -1 defines unlimited amount.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Controlled
component_type.description: In-game component. Currently can't be assigned to entity otherwise.

component_type.name: Craftable
component_type.description:
Defines resources, tools and workstations required to create the entity. Only entities with this component can be crafted. Under [color=192,176,27,255]tools[/color] field, select tools and workstations required to make this entity. [color=192,176,27,255]Time[/color] defines the number of turns to create the item. [color=192,176,27,255]Resources[/color] represent a list of [color=192,176,27,255]entities[/color] with their [color=192,176,27,255]amounts[/color] that will be consumed to create the entity with this component.[color=192,176,27,255]Category Id[/color] represents to which building category in building panel this entity belongs.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Crafter
component_type.description: Allows crafting items. Currently works only for Player.

component_type.name: Damage applier
component_type.description:
This component causes damage to be applied when entity with Moveable component enters the tile. It can be useful for traps. It can be configured to apply damage over time effect.
[color=192,176,27,255]Damage[/color] is a range of damage dealt on trigger.
[color=192,176,27,255]Type[/color] defines type of damage dealt.
[color=192,176,27,255]Duration[/color] applies to damage over time, the damage effect will persist for determined number of turns.
[color=192,176,27,255]Tick every[/color] defies how often a damage should be applied.
[color=192,176,27,255]Animation[/color] is the selected animation that will play when entity triggers the damage.
[color=192,176,27,255]Destroy on trigger[/color] determines if entity with this component should be destroyed when damage triggers or stay on map and trigger again.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Dialogues
component_type.description:
This component allows assigning predefined dialogue entries to the entity, allowing it to communicate in certain situations.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Durability
component_type.description:
Durability component defines how long item is going to last when dealing or receiving damage.

Required components: Item, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Effects
component_type.description: In-game component.

component_type.name: Equipment
component_type.description:
Entities with this component can wear and wield items. They can also hold something in their backpack.
Pick one of the available [color=192,176,27,255]slot[/color] and define a decimal [color=192,176,27,255]chance[/color] (e.g. 0.25 = 25%) to spawn the selected [color=192,176,27,255]item[/color] in place. If item is stackable, it's possible to define number of items to spawn under [color=192,176,27,255]stack[/color] field. You can define multiple items for a single slot and determine their spawn chance to sum up to 1.0 (100%), so entity will randomly spawn with one of the items in slot.

Required components: Actor, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: EXP
component_type.description:
This component determines [color=192,176,27,255]level[/color] of the entity and [color=192,176,27,255]experience[/color] gained by player when the entity is killed.

Required components: Health, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Fighter
component_type.description:
This component determines if an entity can be aggressive. Entities without a fighter component will only attempt to run away, which may be common behavior for smaller animals, for example. Names of unarmed attack types can be specified under [color=192,176,27,255]Attack names[/color]. If no attacks are specified then default names are Fist, Kick, Elbow, Knee, so make sure non-humanoid entities have more fitting names of attacks.

Required components: Actor, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Food
component_type.description:
Food reduces hunger in the same amount as [color=192,176,27,255]nutrition[/color] set. [color=192,176,27,255]Raw[/color] and [color=192,176,27,255]poisonous[/color] properties don't affect the game currently.

Required components: Item, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Fuel
component_type.description:
Fuel entities can be used to ignite and fuel fire, making it burn longer.

Required components: Item, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Glyph
component_type.description:
Glyph component is a representation of the entity on the game screen. Pick one of the available symbols and use sliders to select RGB [color=192,176,27,255]color[/color]. Glyphs support animations, you can pick a frame and input time in milliseconds before next frame will appear. Press right mouse click to remove the frame. At least one frame is required for Glyph component to be valid.

Required components: Name.

component_type.name: Glyph top
component_type.description:
Glyph top determines multiple glyphs that are rendered on top of current entity. An example use case is leaves on trees and bushes. You can select multiple glyphs and define multiple [color=192,176,27,255]colors[/color] which will be picked randomly during world generation. The [color=192,176,27,255]count[/color] field determines number of glyphs that will spawn.
[color=192,176,27,255]Max range[/color] is the max radius from the center of tile.
[color=192,176,27,255]Wind[/color] determines if glyphs should be moved around depending on wind direction and strength.
[color=192,176,27,255]Alpha[/color] determines transparency of all glyphs with range between 0 to 255.

Required components: Glyph, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Health
component_type.description:
Health component make the entity destructible. In the [color=192,176,27,255]health[/color] enter how much damage the entity can take before it's destroyed. If [color=192,176,27,255]Position2D[/color] is not assigned to the entity, the [color=192,176,27,255]health[/color] value will always be 1. Other fields of damage type determine entity (often corpse) that will be created when this entity will be destroyed. If you would like to have a single entity independent of damage type dealt, pick a corpse under [color=192,176,27,255]Any[/color] damage type. [color=192,176,27,255]Can Regenerate Health[/color] checkbox can be unchecked to disabled natural over time regeneration of actor entity.

Required components: Name

component_type.name: Height
component_type.description:
Height component defines how high the entity is when displayed in 3d mode. It's a used to display walls, trees, tables and other stuff. If the entity has a [color=192,176,27,255]Background[/color] component, that component will be used to determine height. If entity doesn't have it, then [color=192,176,27,255]Glyph[/color] component is used.

Required components: Position2D, Name, Background or Glyph

component_type.name: Humanoid
component_type.description:
Humanoids can drag items and keep information about race and profession internally. Editable setting is [color=192,176,27,255]gender[/color] which is only used to generate entity description in [color=192,176,27,255]Name[/color] component during world creation. By default all genders are considered, but some characters might be restricted to male or female.

Required components: Position2D, Name

component_type.name: In progress
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Item
component_type.description:
Items can be of different type. Some types of entities may fit into a different [color=192,176,27,255]Equipment[/color] slots. Item types display in item tooltips and may convey extra information to the players. Items also have an image that is displayed in the equipment screen.

Required components: Position2D, Name

component_type.name: Known by player
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Light
component_type.description:
Light source component generates light at the position of entity. [color=192,176,27,255]Light range[/color] determines radius in tiles of the light. [color=192,176,27,255]Light duration[/color] defines how long the light source will last, with -1 for infinite duration. The entity may also have a different image when it's lit.

Required components: Position2D, Name

component_type.name: Liquid
component_type.description:
Liquid source component defines a liquid of specified [color=192,176,27,255]type[/color] and [color=192,176,27,255]limit[/color]. Infinite source of liquid can be defined by setting limit to -1.

Required components: Position2D, Name

component_type.name: Location objective
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Map connector
component_type.description:
Map connector allows the player to move between different z-levels. A flight of stairs may have value 1 to go to the map directly above or -1 to go directly below.

Required components: Position2D, Name

component_type.name: Member
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Minimap Pixel
component_type.description: Color of the pixel this entity will have when drawn on minimap and world map.

component_type.name: Mouseovered
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Move animation
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Moveable
component_type.description:
Moveable component is required for entities to move around. [color=192,176,27,255]Value[/color] defines the cost of movement in turns, so higher value means the entity is slower.

Required components: Actor, Artificial Intelligence, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Name
component_type.description:
Name is a required component for every entity. [color=192,176,27,255]Name[/color] field should be the entity name, while the description is the extra details to help players imagine what they see.
[color=192,176,27,255]Description[/color] may be generated procedurally if you enter 'Placeholder' into the description and certain conditions are met. Entity requires a certain tag to have a description generated too. Supported tags are Demon, Dwarf, Elf, Fishman, Goblin, Human, Lich, Orc, Rasimi, Reptilion, Satyr, and Troglodyte.

Required components: None.

component_type.name: On destruction
component_type.description:
On destruction component triggers a special effect that may have a global or local scope when entity dies.

Required components: Position2D, Name, Health.

component_type.name: On enter
component_type.description:
On enter component triggers an [color=192,176,27,255]ability[/color] when entity enters the tile with this component.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Openable
component_type.description:
Openable component is used commonly for doors. Its purpose is to replace some elements of the entity depending on its state - either closed or opened. Block light, Collidable, Description, Glyph, and Height are available components that will toggle values with base entity components. Settings defined in this component are for opened entity.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Overlay
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Ownership
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Party
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Persona
component_type.description:
Characters with Persona component are global NPCs. Occupation will display in the character's tooltip.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Player
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Position
component_type.description:
All advanced entities required Position2D component. Without this component, you can create only a limited entity that serves as a background tile like grass or dirt. It's used this way for optimization. Components available without the Position2D component are Name, Background, Height, Health, Collidable, Block light, Random Resource.

Required components: None.

component_type.name: Producer
component_type.description:
Producer component allows creating any entity as a result of an action that player or AI entity can take. 

Required components: Position2D.

component_type.name: Random Resource
component_type.description:
Random resource component allows placing multiple new entities in range, randomly during world generation.
[color=192,176,27,255]Entities[/color] are a list entities that may spawn on the spot.
[color=192,176,27,255]Spawn at entities[/color] is optional and may restrict where entities will spawn - for example spawn the entities only on grass tiles.
[color=192,176,27,255]Amount between[/color] defines range that will be used to generate the count of the entities.
[color=192,176,27,255]Distance between[/color] defines distance in tiles (radius) that can be used to define position where entity should spawn.
[color=192,176,27,255]Spawn chance[/color] determines the chance (0.01 = 1%) for entity to spawn at selected spot during world generation.
[color=192,176,27,255]Spawn rolls[/color] determines how many times the spawn attempt should be repeated.
[color=192,176,27,255]Respawn rate[/color] defines time required to another spawn. If not set the spawn happens only during new world generation.
[color=192,176,27,255]Multiply chance[/color] defines percentage (0.01 = 1%) chance to spawn extra entity into adjacent tile.
[color=192,176,27,255]Multiply same[/color] when checked forces the adjacent tile spawn to be exactly the same as the first rolled.

Required components: Name.

component_type.name: Relations
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Resource
component_type.description:
Resources are used in crafting and provide bonuses to base item recipes. The item with Resource component must have item type Resource set up. To add bonuses to weapons, or armors add Weapon and Armor components respectively and add bonuses there.

Required components: Position2D, Name, Item.

component_type.name: Resurrector
component_type.description:
Resurrector component resurrects the entity. For player, it will resurrect in the starting location. If the component is on item, the effect will apply to the owner. Item will be destroyed upon resurrecting the actor. If this component is on Actor, there's no limit to resurrections making the entity immortal.

Required components: Position2D, Name, Item or Actor.

component_type.name: Resting
component_type.description:
Resting place component adds bonus to sleep speed when some entity with [color=192,176,27,255]Stamina[/color] component sleeps on top of it. The standard resting bonus is 100, so entities with this component should have a higher value unless some area is supposed to have negative resting bonus.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Seen
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Shake
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Sight
component_type.description:
Sight defines a range of visible tiles for Actor entity. [color=192,176,27,255]Range[/color] field defines how far the entity will see during the day if no [color=192,176,27,255]infravision[/color] is set. If [color=192,176,27,255]infravision[/color] is selected, the entity will see at full sight during the night, but only half range during the day.

Required components: Actor, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Skills
component_type.description:
Skills component allows entity to know specific skill(s) at defined level range, and to assign milestones to entities.
This allows entity to:
Act like a teacher if it's not aggresive to player.
Have milestones assigned that can grant passives, active abilities or specific production actions to entity.

Required components: None.

component_type.name: Stack
component_type.description:
Stackable component allows multiple items of the same type to occupy the same space in inventory screen.

Required components: Item, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Stamina
component_type.description:
Stamina component adds stamina and fatigue to Actors forcing them to rest and sleep to regain it after its consumed with actions or movement.

Required components: Actor, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Statistics
component_type.description:
Statistics like Strength, Dexterity, Endurance, Intelligence, and Willpower apply to a multitude of systems in Soulash, for more information check New Game Screen, or Character Sheet in-game. Statistics apply to items and actors. When the Statistics component combines with the Item component, statistic values add to the entity that wears the item. A range of values determines every statistic in this component. During world generation, an algorithm determines a value between the two assigned here.

Required components: Item or Actor, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Summon
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Tags
component_type.description:
Tags define multiple things in the game. They mark entities, and ability effects may trigger based on selected tags. During world generation, entities have races assigned based on these tags. The chosen race may then replace 'Placeholder' description procedurally.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Temporary
component_type.description:
Temporary component means that something will exist in the world for a limited time. [color=192,176,27,255]Time[/color] is defined in turns and will start counting from the moment the entity is created in the world.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Tool
component_type.description:
Tool determines workstations or tools for crafting and their quality.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Transform liquid
component_type.description:
Liquid in Soulash can have two states. One state is a liquid inside a container - it can be drunk or used for cleaning. The other state is covering other entities - in which case the entity may turn into another entity. A blood entity is an actual example with five different entities that get replaced through this component. [color=192,176,27,255]After limit[/color] of liquid is reached the entity gets [color=192,176,27,255]swapped to[/color] selected entity. The covered liquids have percentage values between 1 and 100, while liquid sources measure their limit in consumable units.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Transformed
component_type.description: Internal in-game component.

component_type.name: Usable
component_type.description:
Actors can perform the 'use' action on entities with this component. Use action destroys the item (only if it's Stackable) and applies the [color=192,176,27,255]ability[/color] effect, provides access to [color=192,176,27,255]recipe[/color] or increases the [color=192,176,27,255]skill[/color] potential by 1.

Required components: Item, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Value
component_type.description:
Higher values make the item rarer. This component is currently disabled.

Required components: Item, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Water
component_type.description:
Water component allows the use of 'fishing' skill on the entity to catch fish. [color=192,176,27,255]Swimming[/color] field is currently not used in-game, but will determine if passing the tile requires swimming which will introduce new mechanics in the future. [color=192,176,27,255]Fishing[/color] field toggles the ability to catch fish. [color=192,176,27,255]Fishing during the day and night[/color] determine what kind of fishes can be caught on the tile during certain parts of the day.

Required components: Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Weapon
component_type.description:
Weapon component can be assigned to entity to create a damage dealing item. All weapons should have a selected [color=192,176,27,255]type[/color] that determine hit chance calculation from wielder statistics for example.
[color=192,176,27,255]Two-handed[/color] checkbox will block the second hand when weapon is wielded.
[color=192,176,27,255]Range[/color] higher than 1 allows entities to use 'shoot' action that may either throw the weapon or use ammunition to make a ranged attack.
[color=192,176,27,255]Speed[/color] defines how long in turns an attack action will require time. The standard speed is 1.0 which is equal to a turn, but the speed can take a decimal time. For example many daggers have 0.8 attack speed.
Every point of [color=192,176,27,255]hit bonus[/color] increases the sum of hit chance by 10%, while every point of [color=192,176,27,255]parry bonus[/color] reduce the chance of successful incoming hits by 10%. [color=192,176,27,255]Attack animation[/color] allows assigning unique animations for attacks done with the weapon.

Required components: Item, Position2D, Name.

component_type.name: Weight
component_type.description:
Higher weight makes items more difficult to pick up. It also affects stamina consumption when dragging the item.

Required components: Item, Position2D, Name.

input_state.name: Ranged Attack
input_state.description: Press `::shoot_key` to attack with a ranged weapon.

input_state.name: Crafting
input_state.description: Press `::crafting_key` to open crafting menu and create new items.

input_state.name: World Map
input_state.description: Press `::map_key` to display map of the known world

input_state.name: Building
input_state.description: Press `::build_key` to display list of available buildings.

input_state.name: Skill Book
input_state.description: Press `::skills_key` to display your skill progression and list of available abilities and assign them to hotkeys.

input_state.name: Drink
input_state.description: Press `::drink_key` and select liquid source to drink from it.

input_state.name: Rest
input_state.description: Press `::rest_key` to wait for a turn. This action will regain stamina at the cost of fatigue if possible. You can rest with max stamina to pass 1 turn.

input_state.name: Rest Until Healed
input_state.description:
Press `::rest_until_healed_key` to rest until fully healed. This action will regain stamina at the cost of fatigue if possible.

Character gains a bonus to health regeneration while performing this action based on the duration of rest, with a maximum value based on endurance.

input_state.name: Sleep
input_state.description:
Press `::sleep_key` to sleep and reduce fatigue.

Character gains bonus to health regeneration while performing this action based on the duration of rest, with a maximum value based on endurance.

input_state.name: Move Up or Down
input_state.description: Press `::move_up_down_key` to move to a different location.

input_state.name: Tutorial
input_state.description: Press `::tutorial_key` to display tutorials explaining game.

input_state.name: Clean Yourself
input_state.description: Press `::clean_key` and select water to clean yourself up.

input_state.name: Drag Item
input_state.description:
Press `::drag_key` and select an item on the ground to drag it. Press it again to stop dragging the item.
Dragging affects your speed and stamina consumption on movement.

input_state.name: Open or Close
input_state.description: Press `::open_close_key` and select a target to open or close it.

input_state.name: Sleep Until Morning
input_state.description: Character sleeps until early morning, reducing fatigue, and gaining bonus to health regeneration while performing this action based on the duration of rest, with a maximum value based on endurance.

input_state.name: Sleep Until Evening
input_state.description: Character sleeps until late evening, reducing fatigue, and gaining bonus to health regeneration while performing this action based on the duration of rest, with a maximum value based on endurance.

input_state.name: Converse
input_state.description: Press `::conversation_key` to attempt a conversation with a selected being.

input_state.name: Pickup Item
input_state.description: Pickup one of the items in this position.

history_events.format: ::name died at the age of ::age due to ::death_cause::death_by in ::settlement.
::name succeeded ::related_entity as the head of the ::family family.
::name becomes the head of the ::family family.
::name becomes the new leader of the ::settlement.
::name succeeded ::related_entity as the leader of the ::state state.
::company has been completely eliminated in the battle of ::battle.
::name becomes the leader of the ::state state.
::settlement settlement fell into ruin when its inhabitants perished.
::settlement settlement has been re-settled once more by the ::family family.
::name funded the establishment of a new settlement ::settlement.
::company was established in ::settlement, with ::name as its leader.

passive_effects.description: ::value% chance to perform a basic attack with one of the wielded weapons.

passive_effects.description: If the target has ::value, its effect is increased by ::secondary_value%.

passive_effects.description: Parry +::value%.

passive_effects.name: Extra Equip Slot
passive_effects.description: You gain additional equipment slot for ::value.

passive_effects.name: Resource Discovery
passive_effects.description: You can now spend a few hours to discover a resource on the World Map.

passive_effects.name: Movement Stamina
passive_effects.description: Stamina cost to move ::value%.

passive_effects.name: Points of Interest
passive_effects.description: You can now ask innkeepers and hunters to discover Points of Interest close to their settlement.

passive_effects.name: Sleep Faster
passive_effects.description: Fatigue Recovery during sleep is quicker by ::value%.

passive_effects.name: Area Maps
passive_effects.description: When you enter a new region of z-level ::value, the region map for that level becomes fully discovered.

passive_effects.name: Effect Immunity
passive_effects.description: You're immune to ::value effects.

passive_effects.name: Parry Counter
passive_effects.description: Max Parry Counter +::value. Parry counters reset when you perform an attack.

passive_effects.name: Dodge
passive_effects.description: You have ::value% chance to dodge basic attacks.

passive_effects.name: See Behind

passive_effects.name: No Fall Damage
passive_effects.description: You're immune to fall damage.

passive_effects.name: Unlock Production Action
passive_effects.description: Unlocks action ::value.

passive_effects.name: Thorns From Missing Health
passive_effects.description: Gain +1 Thorns for every ::value% of missing health.

passive_effects.name: Carry Capacity
passive_effects.description: Carry Capacity +::value.

passive_effects.name: Max Health
passive_effects.description: Max Health is increased by +::value%. Applies to base Health and Health gained from Endurance.

passive_effects.name: Max Stamina
passive_effects.description: Max Stamina +::value.

passive_effects.name: Health Regeneration Tick
passive_effects.description: Health Regeneration Time -::value%.

passive_effects.name: Attack Speed
passive_effects.description: Speed up basic attacks by ::value.

passive_effects.name: Movement Penalty
passive_effects.description: Movement penalty from rough terrain is reduced by ::value%.

passive_effects.name: Production Action Speed
passive_effects.description: Speed of ::value action is reduced by ::secondary_value%.

passive_effects.name: Knockback Immunity
passive_effects.description: You are immune to knockback effects.

passive_effects.name: Heal On Kill
passive_effects.description: Heal for ::value% of the health of the killed Actor.

passive_effects.name: Dual Wield 2-Handers
passive_effects.description: You can wield 2-handed weapons in both hands.

passive_effects.name: Dueling Tactician
passive_effects.description: You can see the stamina of hurt beings under their health bar.

passive_effects.name: Target Resistance
passive_effects.description: Your target resistance to ::value damage is reduced by ::secondary_value%.

passive_effects.name: Self Resistance
passive_effects.description: Your resistance to ::value damage is increased by ::secondary_value%.

passive_effects.name: Extra Damage Under Effect
passive_effects.description: Increased damage by ::secondary_value% to targets affected by ::value effect.

passive_effects.name: Light Source
passive_effects.description: You always emit light in the range of ::value.

passive_effects.name: Acquire Tool
passive_effects.description: You gain the ::value tool with quality ::secondary_value.

passive_effects.name: Gain Stamina On Death Under Effect
passive_effects.description: When anyone in the region dies while having active ::value effect, you regain ::secondary_value stamina.

passive_effects.name: Damage Type Reduction
passive_effects.description: You reduce any ::value damage done to you by ::secondary_value%.

passive_effects.name: Spread Entity
passive_effects.description: Any ::value entity in the range of 10 tiles has a ::secondary_value% chance to duplicate into an adjacent tile.

passive_effects.name: Discovery Range
passive_effects.description: You discover special locations in range of ::value instead of 1.

passive_effects.name: Reveal Discovery
passive_effects.description: The details of discovered locations during world travels are immediately revealed.
